english: tsab-tsáb (tshab tshab), mig tsab-tsáb byéd-pa (mig tshab tshab byed pa) to blink or twinkle with the eyes C., also W.

1. mostly with čé (che), čén-po (chen po), very great, very much, sdig-pa tsabs-čé-bar dug (sdig pa tshabs che bar dug) it proves a very great sin, mgó-bo ₒkor tsabs-čé-na (mgo bo 'khor tshabs che na) when much dizziness intervenes Lt.; *t'ím-dhaṅ-gal tsab čén-po* (*khrims dang 'gal tshab chen po*) C., great, serious transgression; ₒgal-tsabs-čan ('gal tshabs can) sinning heinously. - 2. tsábs-pa (tshabs pa) and -po (-po) Cs., who also designates it as resp., peril, fear, sin (rather questionable); difficulty, trouble (might perh. be more adequate); búd-med ₒó-tsabs-la p̥aṅ (bud med 'o tshabs la phan) Wdṅ. it is of use in milk-diseases of the women.

(cf. tsóm-pa (tshom pa), té-tsom (the tshom)) doubt, hesitation, wavering, tsam-tsúm byéd-pa (tsham tshum byed pa) to doubt, hesitate, waver; tsam-tsúm-čan (tsham tshum can), tsam-me-tsom-mé (tsham me tshom me) doubtful, wavering, undecided, p̥aṅ-tsúṅ p̥yág-la tsam-me-tsom-mér lús-pai tse (phan tshun phyag la tsham me tshom mer lus pa'i tshe) whilst both of them were uncertain as to saluting (who should salute first) Pth.

1. also tser (tsher) time Pth. vulgo; tsar-yčíg (tshar gcig) one time, once; tsar yčíg-la (tshar gcig la) also = srib-yčíg-la (srib gcig la) in one moment; tsar ysum (tshar gsum) threefold, in three specimens, copies Tar.; tsar bźi (tshar bzhi) Dzl. 72, 8, in four divisions, sorts, qualities (?) - 2. also tsar-tsar (tshar tshar) ends of threads, fringes, in webs, ka-tsár (ka tshar) Ld. also ru-tsár (ru tshar) fringes at the beginning, poṅ-tsar (pon tshar) at the end of a web Cs. - 3. thin strips of cane, for wicker-work, tsar-zám (tshar zam) cane-bridge C. - 4. tsar-slág (tshar slag) v. tsa-ru (tsha ru). - 5. v. ₒtsár-ba ('tshar ba).
officinal plant in Lh., Carduus nutans, but not agreeing with the description in Wdṅ.

1. provinc. also tsol (tshol), wood, grove, as a place for hunting and recreation, tsal stúg-po (tshal stug po) Dzl.; nags-tsál (nags tshal) id.; garden, mé-tog-gi (me tog gi) flower-garden Ph.; tsal yaṅ-tse (tshal yang tse) (Chin.) C. kitchen-garden. - 2. smyu-gui-tsal (smyu gu'i tshal) one kind of the fabulous food of man in the primitive world Glr.; also the 'unploughed rice' is called ₒbras sa-lu-tsal ('bras sa lu tshal). - 3. v. mtsal (mtshal).

(Sch. tsal-ba? (tshal ba?)) 1. also śiṅ-tsal (shing tshal) chip (of wood), splinter, nón-po (rnon po) a sharp, piercing splinter Dzl.; billet Glr.; thin board, veneer etc.; shiver, fragment, tsál-pa bdún-du gas (tshal pa bdun du gas) Dzl.; tsál-bu (tshal bu) dimin., small chip or shiver W.: *tsál-bu t̥oṅ soṅ* (*tshal bu thong song*) a small piece is broken out. - 2. bunch, of flowers, of ears of corn etc., a lock of hair cut off W.

vulgo for dro (dro), breakfast, tsal-ma za-ba (tshal ma za ba) to breakfast, tsál-ma zá-ba-rnams (tshal ma za ba rnams) 'companions at a great man's table' (?) Cs.; tsál-mai lam (tshal ma'i lam) = tsa-lám (tsha lam) v. tsá-ba (tsha ba) extr.; tsal bóg-pa (tshal bog pa) = dro btáb-pa (dro btab pa) to make a morning-halt on a journey; tsal-rtíṅ (tshal rting) the time from breakfast till dinner, opp. to sná-dro (snga dro), q. v.

(tsás-po (tshas po) Cs.) 1. W. for tsal (tshal) garden, tsas-skór (tshas skor), tsás-kaṅ (tshas khang) garden-bed, tsás-mkan (tshas mkhan) gardener. - 2. of a woman in child-birth: tsas-kyis yso (?) (tshas kyis gso) Med.
(or tsi-rka? (tshi rka?)) C. furrow in a ploughed field.

1. kernel or nut contained in the stone of a stone-fruit, kám-bui (kham bu'i) of an apricot Lt., C. (W.: *rtsi-gu* (*rtsi gu*)). - 2. Ld. a large muller or grinding-stone = ju-lúm (ju lum); musket-ball, bullet.

C., W. *tsi* (*tshi*) tough, viscous, sticky matter, esp. clammy dirt, e.g. in the wool of sheep; tsi dám-po (tshi dam po) solid dirt, bád-kaṅ-gyi tsi-ba (bad kan gyi tshi ba) Med. tenacious slime; tsí(-ba)-čan (tshi(-ba) can) sticky, clammy, dirty; *tsi-du* (*tshi du*) W. dirty, unclean, filthy, esp. in a religious sense, = *kyug-ṭho* (*kyug tho*) C.; *ñe zúg-po tsi-du soṅ* (*nye zug po tshi du song*) says a girl euphemistically for: I have the menses.

1. word, in its strict sense, bde-bar yśégs-pa ni (bde bar gshegs pa ni) bde-ba daṅ yśegs-pai tsig ynyis-las med (bde ba dang gshegs pa'i tshig gnyis las med), bde-bar yśégs-pa (bde bar gshegs pa) are only
